title: The 'if-else' Statement
description: Provide a backup plan for your decisions.

slides.title: The Backup Plan 🛡️
slides.content:
## When the Condition is False

What if the student's score is NOT greater than 50?

If we just use an `if` statement, nothing happens. To provide an alternative action when the condition is **false**, we attach an **`else`** block.

quizData.question: When does the code inside an `else` block execute?

options.text: When the `if` condition evaluates to true
When the `if` condition evaluates to false

quizData.explanation: `else` strictly acts as the backup path if the `if` requirement is not met.

slides.title: Syntax Rules 📐
slides.content:
Go is very picky about where the `else` keyword goes.

It **must** be on the exact same line as the closing curly brace `}` of the `if` block.

`} else {`

quizData.question: Which formatting is valid Go syntax?
quizData.explanation: Because Go inserts semicolons automatically, putting `else` on a new line causes the compiler to break.
